rulings.Case Title: ROBERT LUNDQUIST VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Default Judgement/Leave To File Said Motion Of Specifically Appearing Intervenor Fireman'S Fund Insurance Company On Behalf Of Its Suspended Insured, Defendant Associated Insulation Of California, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 3, 2016 in Department 503, Line 1. Fireman's Fund Insurance Company's motion for leave to file motion to set aside default judgment is granted. Fireman's Fund is allowed to bring said motion. (See Western Heritage Ins. Co. v. Superior Court (2011) 199 Cal.App.4th 1196.) Fireman's Fund's motion to set aside default judgment is granted. The default judgment entered against defendant Associated Insulation of California on September 10, 2015 is set aside pursuant to C.C.P. § 473.5 and pursuant to the court's inherent, equitable power to set aside a default judgment on the ground of mistake. Relief is not authorized pursuant to C.C.P. § 473(b) because the motion was filed more than six months after entry of default judgment. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/GLW)
